sources.claim: Python subprocess supports timeout but process creation cannot always be interrupted immediately, requiring wrapper-level budget semantics.
Python warns shell=True invokes a shell and shifts shell metacharacter safety to the application.
OWASP recommends hardcoded commands/options and strict validation/allowlisting for OS command execution.
Semgrep treats subprocess shell usage and dangerous subprocess patterns as command-injection audit targets.

stage_binding: Evidence supports Stage4 policy: route live governance command execution through a bounded wrapper, block direct live subprocess invocations, allow wrapper internals, and warn rather than rewrite third-party/snapshot/test code.
